notes: The extension runs on <all_urls> and injects spoofing code into page contexts.
The Rust core is a policy engine and logging layer that must remain browser-agnostic.
The repository primarily handles privacy-sensitive persona and host data rather than traditional secrets.

rule_rationale.architecture_boundaries: The repo has a stable split between the Rust core and MV3 extension, so source-level coupling should stay explicit and browser APIs must not leak into core.
rule_rationale.trust_boundary_validation: The extension resolves personas for internet-facing pages using storage and runtime messages, so hostnames and policy config must be validated before use.
rule_rationale.sensitive_logging: The project processes privacy-sensitive persona and host data, so logs must avoid raw spoofed headers, full config dumps, and page-context leakage.
rule_rationale.observability_minimums: Not enabled because this repo is a client-side tool and library, not a production service with centralized telemetry expectations.
rule_rationale.risky_change_notes: Keep diff-aware change notes active for sensitive future changes in auth, security, networking, persistence, or migrations.

allowed_log_exceptions: core/src/logging.rs logs host, persona_id, and headers_rewritten only.
extension/src/background.ts may emit high-level info or warnings without config dumps.
extension/src/content.ts may emit high-level persona match events without raw spoofed values.
extension/ui/popup.ts may surface runtime errors through console.error for debugging.
